source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Pipeline Rupture

actions_taken: Pressure safety low (PSL) for KAA-2000 tripped, initiating shut-in of pipeline at WD Block 152
Operators flew over WD Block 133 F and spotted sheen
Closed shutdown valves (SDVs) and manual block valves on two pipelines at WD Block 133 F
Secured all production casing valves on WD Block 133 F
Divers from DSV Joanne Morrison conducted survey of pipeline damage on September 11, 2018
BSEE onsite investigation conducted at WD Block 152 A on September 21, 2018
event.summary: On September 9, 2018, at 1130 hours, a trawling vessel snagged a bi-directional pipeline (Segment No. 15815) in the Gulf of Mexico, causing it to rupture and release approximately 0.8 gallons of hydrocarbon. The pipeline failed due to excessive stresses imposed when the trawler's net snagged on an 8-inch to 6-inch tie-in spool and dragged it until failure. The tie-in spool, installed in 2007 as part of a Right-of-Way modification, was not sufficiently buried or covered as required by regulations. The incident resulted in an estimated $2,225,000 in property damage.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends that the BSEE GOMR Pipeline Section consider this incident for regulatory review and/or action

hazards.name: Trawling Vessel Operations in Pipeline Area

threats.name: Trawl Net Snagging on Pipeline Infrastructure

consequences.name: Pipeline Rupture and Loss of Containment

controls.name: Pipeline Burial/Cover Protection
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent trawl net contact with pipeline infrastructure
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement 30 CFR 250.1003(a)(2) mandates at least 3 feet of cover for pipeline tie-ins and similar obstructive items

supporting_text: Neither the application nor the completion report contains any indication that the spool was buried or covered
30 CFR 250.1003(a)(2) states that pipeline valves, taps, tie-ins, capped lines, and repaired sections that could be obstructive shall be provided with at least 3 feet of cover
The BSEE GOMR Pipeline Section does not contain any approved departures for this pipeline segment

controls.name: Pressure Safety Low (PSL) Alarm and Automatic Shutdown
controls.barrier_role: Detection and automatic response to pressure loss indicating pipeline failure
controls.lod_basis: Automatic safety system designed to detect pressure anomalies and initiate controlled shutdown

supporting_text: At approximately 1130 hours, on September 9, 2018, the pressure safety low (PSL) for KAA-2000 tripped, initiating a shut-in of the pipeline at WD Block 152

controls.name: Manual Shutdown Valves (SDVs) and Block Valves
controls.barrier_role: Manual isolation of affected pipeline segments to contain hydrocarbon release
controls.lod_basis: Standard operational procedure for isolating damaged pipeline sections

supporting_text: At 1200 hours, operators closed the shutdown valves (SDVs) and manual block valves on the two pipelines at WD Block 133 F

controls.name: Production Casing Valve Isolation
controls.barrier_role: Isolation of well production to prevent further hydrocarbon release
controls.lod_basis: Standard well isolation procedure following pipeline failure

supporting_text: They also secured all production casing valves on WD Block 133 F

controls.name: Right-of-Way Permit and Regulatory Approval Process
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure pipeline installations comply with burial/cover requirements
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory framework requiring approval of pipeline installations and modifications

supporting_text: The BSEE GOMR Pipeline Section provided documentation from the approved ROW permit application
Neither the application nor the completion report contains any indication that the spool was buried or covered
The operator failed to sufficiently cover the tie-in spool without obtaining Regional Supervisory approval

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
